I’ll review the actual patch and run the relevant quality checks (tests/lints/build), then map findings directly to each required criterion.

I found substantial proof additions in `Progress.lean` and `MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean`; next I’ll validate by building/tests and then assess whether they fully satisfy the issue’s exact success claim (`base^exp` in Montgomery form).

## Success criteria from issue
The core criterion is **not fully met**.  
`mod_pow_mont_correct` proves equality to a new recursive helper `mont_pow_loop_spec` (`MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean:1178-1184`), but I found no theorem connecting that spec to `base^exp * R (mod P)` (or equivalent Montgomery exponent semantics). `mont_pow_loop_spec` is only referenced internally (`...:960-1184`), so the requested end-to-end statement is missing.

## Separation of concerns
Pass. Changes are confined to `proofs/` (`Progress.lean`, `MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean`), with no `gf2-core` ↔ `gf2-coding` layering violation.

## Functional paradigm and performance
Pass for scope. Proof-side additions mirror algorithm structure and use existing specs; no kernel/perf regressions introduced.

## Correctness
Partial. Loop/progress reasoning is strong (`@[progress] mod_pow_mont_loop_progress`, `Progress.lean:520+`; loop invariant machinery at `MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean:982+`), including the `if exp2 > 0` squaring guard.  
But final mathematical target (`base^exp` in Montgomery form) is not proven.

## Testing / linting
Could not execute `cargo`/`lake` commands in this environment (permission denied). Available gate evidence: `lake-build` is passed; `doc-review` remains pending in issue metadata. No run_history items to verify.

## Unsafe isolation
Pass. No new unsafe code; changes are Lean proofs only.

## Documentation
Mixed. Proof comments are present, but no final theorem documenting/establishing the explicit `base^exp` Montgomery semantic statement requested by the issue.

## Prior review feedback
`run_history` is empty; nothing to reconcile.

## Issue dependencies
No dependencies listed; nothing blocked upstream.

VERDICT: FAIL

Gate result: FAILED
